title: Rubber Parts Periodic Inspection Items and Master Power Inspection

crossRefs.label: Inspect master power function and airtightness.
If the brake hose is damaged or leaking, replace it with a new one.
Disassemble and inspect the calipers, and be sure to replace the rubber parts specified as being replaced at disassembly, such as piston seals and dust covers, with new ones.
Disassemble and inspect the wheel cylinders, and be sure to replace any rubber parts that are specified to be replaced at the time of disassembly with new ones.

blocks.text: Rubber Parts Periodic Inspection Items and Master Power Inspection
1. Rubber Parts Periodic Inspection Items and Master Power Inspection
*1: Inspect whether brake performance or braking force meets the specified values.
*2: If the lower part of the master power and master cylinder connection shows evidence of having been wetted by brake fluid but is dry in the present condition, this is not abnormal.

items.text: Brakes Pedal and how well the brakes work. *1 and if there is any abnormality, check the Inspect master power function and airtightness.

items.text: If the malfunction is caused by the master power, Replace master power.

Check that there is no difference in pedal stroke when the brake pedal is depressed slowly or rapidly.
Check that there is no difference in pedal stroke when the brake pedal is depressed slowly or rapidly.
Visually inspect the master cylinder for damage and leaks.
Visually inspect the master cylinder for damage and leaks.
Abnormal pedal operation, change (difference) in pedal stroke, or damage to master cylinder, fluid leakage *2 If, for example, there is a Replace the master cylinder.
Visually inspect the brake hose for damage and leaks.

refs.label: If the brake hose is damaged or leaking, replace it with a new one.

items.text: Visually inspect the brake hose for damage and leaks.
If the brake hose is damaged or leaking, replace it with a new one.

Visually inspect calipers for damage and fluid leaks.

refs.label: Disassemble and inspect the calipers, and be sure to replace the rubber parts specified as being replaced at disassembly, such as piston seals and dust covers, with new ones.

items.text: Visually inspect calipers for damage and fluid leaks.

If the pedal operates in an abnormal manner, or if the caliper has abnormal drag, damage, or leaks, etc, Disassemble and inspect the calipers, and be sure to replace the rubber parts specified as being replaced at disassembly, such as piston seals and dust covers, with new ones.

refs.label: Disassemble and inspect the calipers, and be sure to replace the rubber parts specified as being replaced at disassembly, such as piston seals and dust covers, with new ones.

items.text: Brakes Pedal and how well the brakes work. *1 Inspect the
Brakes Pedal and how well the brakes work. *1 Inspect the
Visually inspect wheel cylinders for damage and leaks.
Visually inspect wheel cylinders for damage and leaks.
Turn over the upper part of the dust cover with your finger and check for brake fluid in the dust seal.

refs.label: Disassemble and inspect the wheel cylinders, and be sure to replace any rubber parts that are specified to be replaced at the time of disassembly with new ones.

items.text: Turn over the upper part of the dust cover with your finger and check for brake fluid in the dust seal.

If the pedal action is abnormal, if the wheel cylinder is damaged or leaking, or if there is brake fluid in the dust seal, Disassemble and inspect the wheel cylinders, and be sure to replace any rubber parts that are specified to be replaced at the time of disassembly with new ones.

refs.label: Disassemble and inspect the wheel cylinders, and be sure to replace any rubber parts that are specified to be replaced at the time of disassembly with new ones.
